findings.description: The PDF presents the data as a compact, single-page wide table with all columns visible horizontally. The HTML conversion stretches the table vertically with much taller rows due to word-wrapping in narrow columns, significantly changing the layout from a compact landscape-oriented table to a very long portrait-oriented table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows all 18 data rows fitting compactly on one page. HTML renders the same content across a very long scrollable page with each row taking significantly more vertical space due to wrapped text in the Owner-Facility and other columns.

findings.description: The table header label 'Owner - Facility' in the PDF is rendered as 'Owner - Facility' in the HTML but with a line break making it appear as 'Owner -' on one line and 'Facility' on another, and the column 'As Built Flow MGD' is similarly reformatted with different line breaks. While data is preserved, the visual presentation of headers differs.
findings.evidence: PDF header row has compact single-line or two-line headers. HTML headers are wrapped differently due to narrower column widths.

findings.description: The 'Total Cases in Previous 12 Months' header column is reformatted with different line breaks in HTML compared to PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Total Cases in Previous 12 Months' displayed compactly. HTML: broken across multiple lines as 'Total Cases in Previous 12 Months'.
